documents.topic: RFC_8259 text-parser hotspots at stdlib-scale inputs; debug vs release framing, allocation reduction, Span-cursor path. v1.2.0 (DECISION): Tier 4 LANDED — Foundation parity achieved (1.02× on bytes, 1.06× on String). Tiers 2/5 remain HELD pending second hot consumer.

Tier-4 architecture design: Span<UInt8>-specialized internal lexer for RFC_8259 to reach ≤1.3× Foundation on stdlib-scale inputs. Enumerates three architectures, recommends Architecture A (internal Span cursor mirroring Binary.Bytes.Input.View, lazy position with cache-on-first-access, dispatch fork in RFC_8259.Decode). v1.0.2 (DECISION): Phase A1 LANDED, A2 measurement gate PASSED — bytes 1.02× Foundation, String 1.06×, both well below 1.3× target. Phase B (arena tree) NOT triggered. Naming refinement (RFC_8259.Span.Lexer over RFC_8259.Lexer.Span) and three optimisations beyond spec recorded in §9.

swift-json v2 value-tree architecture targeting O(1) lookup beating Foundation on lookup-heavy workloads (canonical: symbol-graph oracle's symbol.kind.identifier traversal pattern over 14 552 symbols). Enumerates four architectures: L1 (Ordered dict, Copyable Value — recommended), L2 (~Copyable arena-bound Value tree), L3 (hybrid small/large), L4 (lazy index). Recommends L1: replace RFC_8259.Object._storage with Dictionary<String, Value>.Ordered from swift-dictionary-primitives; preserves entire public API surface; consumes witness-primitive triple transitively via Hash Primitives Standard Library Integration. L2 stays parked as the predecessor's conditional Phase B, re-opens only with a second hot consumer + Buffer.Arena Option A landed. Phased plan: L1-0 verification + lookup-bench mode (½ day), L1-1 storage replacement (3–5 days), L1-2 measurement gate, L1-3 conditional cleanup.

narrativeSections.heading: See also
narrativeSections.body: Modularization audit findings (MOD-010 stdlib extensions, MOD-011 test support) live in `swift-foundations/swift-json/Audits/audit.md` rather than `Research/`; the audit was incorrectly indexed here from 2026-03-20 to 2026-05-13 and has been moved to its correct home.
